title: Error burst in StreamOps pipeline logs
summary: Error rate spiked to 60% across pipeline components. Log search shows NullPointerException and TimeoutException cascading through kafka-consumer and flink-operator. No metric-level anomalies detected; this is a log-only event.
root_cause: Cascading exception chain initiated by intermittent Kafka consumer timeout, propagating through the Flink operator graph as NullPointerExceptions on unhandled null records.
timeline: T+0s: Error burst scenario injected by simulator
T+3s: LogGenerator begins emitting ERROR/WARN severity logs at 60% rate
T+8s: AnomalyDetector error rate counter crosses threshold
T+10s: Agent detects error burst via search_logs and recent events
T+14s: Diagnostic Agent correlates error patterns across components
T+17s: Report Agent classifies as MEDIUM (degraded but not SLA-breaking)

recommended_actions.action: Add null-check guards in Flink operator deserialization path
recommended_actions.rationale: The NullPointerExceptions indicate missing null handling when Kafka returns empty records

recommended_actions.action: Increase Kafka consumer session.timeout.ms from 10s to 30s
recommended_actions.rationale: The initial timeout suggests the consumer is being evicted during brief pauses

monitoring_notes: Monitor error_rate and search for NullPointerException in logs. Error rate should drop below 0.2 within 5 minutes of the burst ending.
